findings.description: learn:execute embeds JSON directly inside single quotes without escaping. A single quote in evidence can break argument parsing and can become a command injection vector depending on Bash() semantics.
findings.title: Shell injection / quoting breakage when passing JSON to learn:update-progress
findings.suggested_issue: Add shared escaping helper (or pass evidence via temp file/base64) and quote all args.

findings.description: learn:execute passes JSON.stringify(profile) inside single quotes without escaping; this is fragile and can fail or be exploitable.
findings.title: Shell injection / quoting breakage when passing profile JSON to learn:write-profile
findings.suggested_issue: Reuse escapeSingleQuotesForShell or move to file-based payloads (data-file).

findings.description: learn:plan runs node with unquoted paths and promotes plan using mv via Bash(). This is not portable and increases injection surface.
findings.title: Unquoted paths and bash-only operations in plan validation pipeline
findings.suggested_issue: Quote paths; avoid mv; prefer cross-platform atomic write/rename (Node or ccw CLI).

findings.snippet: supportsPermissions = nodeMajor >= 22 (else: run without permission flags)
findings.description: mcp-runner uses Node permission model only when Node >= 22; on older versions it runs without permission flags (preload blocks common network APIs but is not a complete sandbox).
findings.title: Untrusted code execution is not strongly sandboxed on Node < 22
findings.suggested_issue: Fail closed on Node < 22 or add a stronger sandbox boundary.

findings.description: State schema allows arbitrary strings and learn:update-state only validates current_phase. Invalid ids can later break workflows and interact poorly with shell interpolation in command docs.
findings.title: learn:update-state allows invalid active_profile_id/active_session_id values
findings.suggested_issue: Validate active_profile_id and active_session_id formats at update-time; reject invalid values.

findings.description: CLI command accepts --file and reads it directly; unlike learn state commands, it does not validate paths under project root.
findings.title: learn:parse-background reads arbitrary file paths without allowlist validation
findings.suggested_issue: Use validatePath() allowlist for file reads (or document that this is trusted-only).
